instructions: Set shoulders down and back before the first rep.
Drive through the mid-hand and keep wrists stacked over elbows.
Lower under control until the handle path stays level with the mid chest.
Press to a strong finish without losing upper-back tension.
tips: Keep your ribcage quiet and let the chest do the work.
Use a 2-3 second lowering phase for cleaner chest loading.
Stop the set when the shoulders roll forward or the press path changes.
common_mistakes: Flaring elbows too early and shifting tension into the shoulders.
Bouncing the bottom position instead of owning the eccentric.
Overextending the lower back to finish the rep.
horizontal_press.summary: A premium upper-body press pattern focused on chest-driven force production with shoulder stability.

instructions: Set the bench angle so the upper chest leads instead of the front delts.
Start with dumbbells stacked over elbows and shoulders packed into the bench.
Lower slightly outside the upper chest with a smooth controlled arc.
Press up and in while keeping the ribs quiet.
tips: Squeeze the handles as you press to improve shoulder stability.
Think elbows 30-45 degrees from the torso rather than flared straight out.
Finish each rep with tension, not momentum.
common_mistakes: Using too steep a bench and turning the move into a shoulder press.
Letting the dumbbells drift behind the elbows at the bottom.
Crashing the weights together at the top.
incline_press.summary: An upper-chest biased press that rewards clean scapular control and smooth dumbbell pathing.

instructions: Set the chest high and keep a soft bend in the elbows.
Open until you feel chest stretch without shoulder glide.
Sweep the arms together in a hugging arc and squeeze the chest at peak contraction.
Keep the rep tempo smooth on both directions.
tips: Think biceps toward biceps instead of hands toward hands.
Shorten the range if the front shoulder takes over.
Use lighter loading and chase constant chest tension.
common_mistakes: Straightening the elbows and turning the move into a press-lever.
Reaching too deep and losing shoulder position.
Rushing the stretch phase.
chest_fly.summary: A chest isolation pattern built around controlled stretch, smooth adduction, and tension quality.

instructions: Set elbows just ahead of the torso and lock them into place.
Extend the arms by driving the handle down without shoulder swing.
Pause in full extension, then return with control to the top.
Keep the torso still and let the elbows dictate the motion.
tips: Use a slightly forward lean only if it helps keep elbows pinned.
Finish with the pinky side of the hand if using a rope.
Choose a load that keeps the triceps hot through the entire range.
common_mistakes: Letting elbows drift forward as fatigue builds.
Using body weight to force the last inches of the rep.
Rebounding the top instead of controlling the return.
tricep_pushdown.summary: A crisp triceps isolation pattern designed for clean elbow extension and repeatable arm tension.

instructions: Set elbows high with ribs stacked over the pelvis.
Lower the implement behind the head without flaring the ribs.
Extend through the elbows until the arms finish long overhead.
Keep the upper arms stable while the forearms move.
tips: Use a split stance if it helps control the torso.
Think about stretching the long head at the bottom rather than forcing range.
Keep neck relaxed and shoulders away from the ears.
common_mistakes: Turning the movement into a standing incline press.
Flaring the elbows wide and losing line of pull.
Hyperextending the low back to finish reps.
overhead_tricep.summary: A long-head triceps movement that pairs stretch loading with overhead shoulder control.

instructions: Brace lightly and flatten the lower ribs before the first rep.
Curl the sternum toward the pelvis without yanking the neck forward.
Pause in the shortened position and breathe out fully.
Lower back down one segment at a time.
tips: Think ribcage down rather than elbows to knees.
Small range with full ab tension beats large sloppy motion.
Exhale hard to finish each rep.
common_mistakes: Pulling on the neck instead of curling through the trunk.
Using momentum and bouncing through the bottom.
Letting hip flexors dominate the movement.
crunch.summary: A simple trunk-flexion movement that emphasizes controlled abdominal shortening.

instructions: Set the torso tall with ribs down and shoulders packed.
Drive elbows toward the ribs while keeping wrists quiet.
Finish near the collarbone or upper chest depending on grip.
Control the return until the lats lengthen fully.
tips: Think elbows down rather than bar down.
Use a slight torso angle only if it keeps tension in the lats.
Pause briefly in the contracted position.
common_mistakes: Leaning too far back and turning the move into a row.
Shrugging into the pull instead of depressing the scapula.
Losing control at the top stretch.
lat_pulldown.summary: A vertical pull pattern built around scapular depression and full lat lengthening.

instructions: Set the chest proud and feet planted before initiating the row.
Pull elbows back along the torso and finish with shoulder blades moving last.
Pause against the body without rounding forward.
Return with reach but keep the lower back neutral.
tips: Lead with elbows, not hands.
Match the handle path to the muscle target: lower for lats, wider for upper back.
Keep the neck long and shoulders away from the ears.
common_mistakes: Using torso swing to start the rep.
Cutting the stretch short on the way forward.
Jamming the shoulders upward at the finish.
seated_row.summary: A horizontal pull pattern that blends lat drive with clean mid-back retraction.

instructions: Hinge slightly with ribs stacked and arms long.
Sweep the handle from shoulder height to the pockets without bending the elbows much.
Pause with the lats shortened and shoulders depressed.
Return slowly until the lats fully lengthen.
tips: Think armpits to hips for clean lat engagement.
Use the torso angle that lets the lats stay in front of the shoulders.
Keep a soft elbow bend but do not row the handle.
common_mistakes: Bending the elbows and turning it into a triceps move.
Snapping the torso to finish the range.
Letting shoulders climb toward the ears.
straight_arm_pushdown.summary: A lat isolation movement that teaches shoulder extension without elbow-driven rowing.

instructions: Set the chest into the pad or hinge and keep the neck quiet.
Open the arms wide with elbows soft and hands following the rear delts.
Pause when the upper arm lines up with the torso.
Return with control and stay out of the traps.
tips: Lead with elbows and think wide arc, not squeeze the hands back.
Use lighter loading and prioritize shoulder position.
Keep ribs down so upper back stays stable.
common_mistakes: Shrugging into the rep.
Bouncing the start position.
Turning the move into a row by bending the elbows too much.
rear_delt_fly.summary: A rear-shoulder isolation movement built for posture, scapular control, and shoulder balance.

instructions: Lock the upper arm near the torso and start from full elbow extension.
Curl the weight by flexing the elbow without torso sway.
Squeeze the biceps at the top for a clean finish.
Lower slower than you lifted.
tips: Keep wrists neutral and grip firm but not death-tight.
Think pinkies slightly up near the top to finish supination.
Stay tall instead of leaning the rep backward.
common_mistakes: Swinging the torso to start the curl.
Letting elbows drift forward and losing tension.
Dropping the eccentric.
bicep_curl.summary: A classic elbow-flexion pattern emphasizing smooth tension and clean arm positioning.

instructions: Start with thumbs-forward grip and elbows fixed near the ribs.
Curl without rotating the handles and keep wrists stacked.
Pause at the top where the forearm is fully folded.
Lower slowly and maintain neutral grip through the bottom.
tips: Think handle to shoulder, not shoulder to handle.
Keep the humerus quiet so the forearm does the work.
Use neutral wrists to bias the brachialis and forearm.
common_mistakes: Turning the curl into a swing.
Losing neutral grip and supinating halfway up.
Rushing the lowering phase.
hammer_curl.summary: A neutral-grip curl that builds arm density through brachialis and forearm emphasis.

instructions: Support the forearms and let the wrists hang free of the bench edge.
Open the fingers slightly to lengthen the flexors, then curl the bar into the palm.
Finish by flexing the wrists without lifting the elbows.
Lower under complete control.
tips: Use shorter ranges and higher quality reps.
Let the hands open just enough to create a stretch, not a grip reset.
Keep shoulders relaxed and torso still.
common_mistakes: Moving the elbows instead of the wrists.
Using too much weight and shortening the range.
Bouncing off the bottom stretch.
wrist_curl.summary: A forearm-focused accessory for wrist flexor strength, grip endurance, and arm balance.

instructions: Set forearms on support with palms facing down.
Lift the back of the hand by extending at the wrist.
Pause briefly in the top position.
Lower back into stretch without dropping the implement.
tips: Use lighter weights than you think.
Stay patient at the top and own the contraction.
This works best with strict reps and no elbow movement.
common_mistakes: Using momentum to snap the wrists up.
Shrugging or tensing the neck.
Cutting the eccentric short.
reverse_wrist_curl.summary: A targeted extensor accessory that improves forearm balance and elbow resilience.

instructions: Set the seat so handles begin near chin-to-ear height.
Brace the trunk and press straight overhead through the palm center.
Finish with biceps near ears without shrugging hard into the top.
Lower to a controlled bottom while keeping the forearm vertical.
tips: Keep your chin tucked enough to create a clean press path.
Lock the ribcage down so the shoulders, not the low back, own the motion.
Use a stable tempo and stay out of the traps until the last inch.
common_mistakes: Overarching the back to chase range.
Letting elbows drift too far behind the handle path.
Shrugging aggressively from the start of each rep.
shoulder_press.summary: A vertical pressing movement focused on strong shoulder output with controlled overhead mechanics.

instructions: Start with soft elbows and hands slightly in front of the body.
Raise the arms out and away until elbows reach shoulder height.
Lead with the elbows and keep wrists quiet.
Lower slowly to maintain tension.
tips: Think wide reach, not dump the pinkies.
Keep traps quiet and shoulders long.
Small body english is acceptable only after strict reps are gone.
common_mistakes: Shrugging from the first inch.
Swinging the torso to create speed.
Taking the hands too high and losing the lateral delt.
lateral_raise.summary: A shoulder-width builder emphasizing clean abduction and trap restraint.

instructions: Set the cable high enough to pull toward the bridge of the nose or forehead.
Lead with elbows high and finish by rotating the hands apart.
Pause with upper arms level to the floor.
Return slowly while keeping ribs stacked.
tips: Think pull then rotate, not one rushed motion.
Use rope separation at the end to light up the rear shoulder.
Keep the neck neutral and shoulders out of the ears.
common_mistakes: Turning it into a row by dropping the elbows.
Using too much load and losing rotation.
Leaning backward to finish the rep.
face_pull.summary: A shoulder health staple that blends rear-delt work with upper-back control and external rotation.

instructions: Set a tall chest and brace before rotating.
Turn through the ribs instead of only moving the arms.
Keep the pelvis as quiet as possible while the trunk rotates.
Move side to side with equal control.
tips: Smaller controlled turns beat fast sloppy touches.
Exhale as you rotate into the hard side.
Use heel contact or feet up only if you can keep posture.
common_mistakes: Rounding the spine and chasing the floor.
Throwing the weight with the arms.
Letting the hips rock excessively.
russian_twist.summary: A rotational trunk pattern aimed at oblique control rather than speed alone.

instructions: Set elbows under shoulders and drive the floor away.
Brace ribs down and squeeze glutes to flatten the body line.
Breathe shallowly through the brace rather than holding your breath.
Maintain one clean line from head to heels.
tips: Shorter perfect holds are more valuable than long compromised ones.
Reach the forearms forward to increase serratus tension.
Think zipper ribs to pelvis.
common_mistakes: Letting the low back sag.
Piking the hips too high to make the hold easier.
Holding the breath and losing tension rhythm.
plank.summary: A high-value isometric core drill for trunk stiffness, bracing, and shoulder integrity.

instructions: Set the machine axis in line with the knee and the pad just above the heel.
Brace the hips down into the pad before curling.
Drive the heel toward the glute with no hip lift.
Lower fully to lengthen the hamstrings.
tips: Point toes slightly away if it helps reduce calf takeover.
Pause in the squeeze to own the shortened position.
Use complete range before adding load.
common_mistakes: Lifting the hips off the pad.
Rushing the eccentric.
Using too much weight and stopping short of full bend.
leg_curl.summary: A hamstring-focused curl that builds knee flexor strength and posterior chain balance.

instructions: Sit tall and set the pads against the outer thighs.
Drive the knees apart with hips staying square in the seat.
Pause in the open position and squeeze the outer glutes.
Return slowly without slamming the stack.
tips: A slight forward torso lean may improve glute bias.
Keep tension all the way back in.
Use this as control work, not momentum work.
common_mistakes: Throwing the pads apart with speed.
Rounding the low back to chase range.
Relaxing completely between reps.
hip_abduction.summary: A glute medius accessory that supports hip stability, pelvic control, and knee-friendly training.

instructions: Sit upright with pads placed on the inner thigh.
Draw the legs together under control without collapsing the chest.
Pause at the squeeze and keep the pelvis steady.
Return slowly to the stretch.
tips: Think thighs in rather than feet in.
Do not bounce out of the stretch.
Use a range that keeps the pelvis planted.
common_mistakes: Slamming the pads together.
Rounding or rocking the torso.
Using weight that removes end-range control.
hip_adduction.summary: An inner-thigh focused accessory that supports pelvic control and frontal-plane balance.

instructions: Set feet so shins are near vertical at the top.
Brace ribs down before driving through the heels.
Lift the hips until glutes lock out without lumbar extension.
Lower with control and keep the knees tracking straight.
tips: Think tailbone to knees long at the top.
Push the floor away instead of arching up.
Use a brief pause in lockout to own the glute finish.
common_mistakes: Overarching the low back in the top position.
Setting the feet too far away and overusing hamstrings.
Letting knees cave inward.
glute_bridge.summary: A glute-dominant hip extension pattern for posterior chain strength and pelvic control.

instructions: Start with a soft bend at the knee and tension already on the band or cable.
Drive the knee to full extension by squeezing the quad.
Pause in the locked-out position for clean VMO engagement.
Return with the foot flat and hips quiet.
tips: Think knee cap up and thigh tight.
Stay tall and let the quad finish the rep.
Use moderate tension and precise reps.
common_mistakes: Rocking the whole body to create extension.
Snapping the knee instead of controlling the finish.
Letting the arch collapse as you press back.
terminal_knee_extension.summary: A knee-friendly quad drill built for terminal extension control and joint confidence.

instructions: Set the ball of the foot on the platform with a controlled heel drop.
Drive straight up through the big toe and second toe.
Pause in the top squeeze and stay tall through the torso.
Lower slowly into full calf stretch.
tips: Use the longest pain-free range you can control.
Keep pressure even across the forefoot.
Treat each rep as pause-stretch, pause-squeeze.
common_mistakes: Bouncing through the bottom.
Rolling to the outside of the foot.
Cutting the top contraction short.
calf_raise.summary: A lower-leg accessory designed to build calf size, ankle stiffness, and controlled stretch tolerance.

tips: Keep elbows tight to the torso to bias the lower lats.
Stay tall and avoid turning the pull into a row.
Pause at the chest and own the squeeze.

Glute Bridge Machine.equipment: Glute Bridge or Hip Thrust Machine
